Title: Expanding the roles for 2-oxoglutarate-dependent oxygenases in plant metabolism

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: DAO can be targeted to tune auxin homeostasis and thereby architecture or reproductive traits, but deployment requires caution because auxin is a master hormone with broad pleiotropic effects.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DAO oxidizes auxin (IAA) to oxIAA, promoting auxin catabolism and homeostasis; loss of DAO1 elevates auxin conjugates and alters development-related auxin balance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay; mutant analysis/knockout evidence in Arabidopsis; metabolite profiling of auxin and conjugates.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Increased auxin levels; reproductive development context.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reported in reproductive development context in rice; Arabidopsis DAO1 functions broadly in auxin homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Reproductive development-associated in rice; homologs in Arabidopsis linked to auxin-responsive contexts.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a review article summarizing prior work up to 2018; DAO belongs to DOXC clade 2OG oxygenases.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating DAO may broadly perturb embryogenesis, organogenesis, tropisms, apical dominance and regeneration due to auxin imbalance.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DAO enzymes catalyze oxidation of auxin to oxIAA. 2. DAO1 mediates the major auxin oxidation route in Arabidopsis. 3. Disruption of DAO1 strongly increases auxin conjugates, demonstrating a central role in auxin homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: General plant tissues where auxin turnover occurs
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains auxin homeostasis and developmental patterning

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for modifying GA levels and plant stature, but this review provides no new engineering details.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidative steps in gibberellin biosynthesis catalyzed by GA20 oxidase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes gibberellin production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized from prior literature in review; no primary validation in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms pathway intermediate/precursor for active gibberellins
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Listed in review table only.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: GA manipulation often alters height and development.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA20ox is a conserved plant 2ODO in hormone metabolism. 2. It participates in GA biosynthesis. 3. The review uses it as an example of primary metabolic 2ODO function.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune salicylic acid turnover and defense balance.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates salicylic acid at the 3-position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes salicylic acid catabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized from prior literature in review.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Listed in review table only.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could affect immunity-growth balance.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. S3H is a plant 2ODO involved in salicylic acid catabolism. 2. It demonstrates extension of 2ODO roles into defense hormone turnover. 3. It is cited as a representative reaction in the review.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to SA homeostasis and defense modulation

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A candidate for engineering coumarin/furanocoumarin production in medicinal or defense contexts; should be paired with downstream prenylation and tailoring genes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Ortho-hydroxylates 4-coumaroyl-CoA at the 2' position, initiating rearrangement/lactonization to form umbelliferone.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes production of umbelliferone, the entry metabolite toward defensive furanocoumarins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BAC-clone guided gene discovery; recombinant enzyme in vitro assay with 4-coumaroyl-CoA yielding umbelliferone.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified from a BAC clone in parsnip associated with furanocoumarin locus.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PsDIOX was discovered near PT1, a furanocoumarin biosynthetic gene.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced furanocoumarins may raise phototoxicity or affect crop usability.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsDIOX encodes 4-coumaroyl-CoA 2'-hydroxylase activity. 2. The enzyme converts 4-coumaroyl-CoA to umbelliferone in vitro. 3. It provides a key entry step to furanocoumarin biosynthesis in parsnip.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against herbivores/pathogens and environmental interactions

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering scopoletin/coumarin metabolism, potentially affecting defense and iron-related responses.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Ortho-hydroxylates feruloyl-CoA at the 6' position; the product spontaneously isomerizes/lactonizes to scopoletin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes scopoletin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Crystal structure/apo-enzyme modelling; prior biochemical characterization summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review discusses structural basis of catalysis and conserved RXS motif.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered phenylpropanoid flux may affect other defense phenolics.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtF6'H hydroxylates feruloyl-CoA to initiate scopoletin formation. 2. Structural work revealed active-site features supporting 2OG and substrate binding. 3. It exemplifies ortho-hydroxylation chemistry in coumarin metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for opiate pathway engineering in plants or microbes; should be stacked with upstream and downstream morphinan genes and balanced to avoid accumulation of controlled alkaloids/intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-demethylates codeine through hydroxylation and subsequent formaldehyde release.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes morphine pathway progression by converting codeine toward morphine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology-based candidate discovery from transcriptome resources; biochemical functional assays summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Among abundant proteins in alkaloid-synthesizing latex tissue.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review highlights CODM as a landmark example of plant 2ODO-catalyzed O-demethylation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Engineering may alter alkaloid spectra and toxicity; regulatory issues for narcotic products.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CODM is a 2ODO that catalyzes O-demethylation in morphine biosynthesis. 2. Its discovery expanded known chemistry of plant 2ODOs. 3. It is highly relevant for alkaloid pathway engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for broad benzylisoquinoline alkaloid diversification and pathway reconstruction in heterologous systems.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-dealkylates protopine-related alkaloids via hydroxylation followed by formaldehyde release.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to diversification of poppy alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology-based discovery and biochemical assays summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes new poppy 2ODOs dealkylating or demethylenating diverse alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May create complex alkaloid mixtures and metabolic burden.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PODA is a poppy 2ODO involved in alkaloid O-dealkylation. 2. It illustrates broad 2ODO recruitment into benzylisoquinoline metabolism. 3. The enzyme likely acts through hydroxylation and formaldehyde release.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to reduce toxic glycoalkaloids in food crops or enhance defense in non-food contexts.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates 22,26-oxycholesterol at the 16α position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized from prior literature in review.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Listed in review table only.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing activity may elevate toxic antinutritional glycoalkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 16DOX is a specialized-metabolism 2ODO in steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis. 2. It expands the reaction space of plant 2ODOs into sterol-derived defense compounds. 3. It is cited as a representative pathway enzyme in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A target for modifying plant architecture and branching; should be used carefully due to broad strigolactone roles in development and rhizosphere signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes methylcarlactonoate to an unknown product with +16 Da mass increase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for proper control of shoot branching via downstream strigolactone-related metabolite formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics mining; inducible expression analyses; reverse genetics; biochemical evidence summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified through organ-specific transcriptomics in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Arabidopsis organ-specific expression linked to pathway discovery.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Product identity remained unknown in this review; enzyme linked to methylcarlactonoate turnover.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered branching and altered rhizosphere interactions/mycorrhizal or parasitic plant signaling may occur.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for branching-related output
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBO is a newly identified Arabidopsis 2ODO in strigolactone metabolism. 2. It converts methylcarlactonoate to an oxidized product required for shoot branching control. 3. Its discovery came from transcriptomics and reverse genetics.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls shoot branching and plant architecture

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for breeding radish glucosinolate composition, especially glucoraphasatin content and flavor/health traits; favorable for marker-assisted selection or editing of the recessive allele.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely desaturates a glucosinolate precursor to form glucoraphasatin, though a hydroxylated intermediate cannot be excluded.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls accumulation of glucoraphasatin, the major glucosinolate in radish.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; map-based cloning from glucoraphasatin-free radish; phylogenetic analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Radish uniquely accumulates glucoraphasatin at >90% of total glucosinolates; GRS1 belongs to DOXC31.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing glucosinolate profiles may alter flavor, defense, and nutritional/antinutritional properties.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GRS1 was identified as the gene controlling glucoraphasatin accumulation in radish. 2. Map-based cloning from a glucoraphasatin-free mutant revealed a novel 2ODO. 3. The enzyme likely desaturates the precursor to produce glucoraphasatin.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and human health-related isothiocyanate precursor

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for etoposide precursor production; should be stacked with upstream CYPs and O-methyltransferase and downstream CYPs to reconstruct full pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cyclization of (-)-yatein-derived intermediate, likely via hydroxylation followed by dehydration, stereoselective cyclization and rearomatization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of the podophyllotoxin core skeleton and production of (-)-4'-desmethylepipodophyllotoxin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Organ-specific transcriptomics; targeted inducible expression analyses; pathway reconstitution/biochemical functional assays summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms scaffold of target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Wound-inducible expression used for candidate selection.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Tissue-specific and wound-inducible, matching known podophyllotoxin pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts after two CYPs and an O-methyltransferase, before two downstream CYPs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Metabolic burden and potential toxicity of lignan intermediates in heterologous systems.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Cyclized aryltetralin lignan intermediate en route to (-)-4'-desmethylepipodophyllotoxin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 2ODD catalyzes a key oxidative cyclization in podophyllotoxin biosynthesis. 2. Discovery relied on transcriptomic and expression-guided candidate selection. 3. The enzyme is essential for forming the podophyllotoxin skeleton used for etoposide precursor production.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and pharmaceutical precursor for etoposide

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for improving phytosiderophore production and iron acquisition in cereals under deficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation step in mugineic acid/phytosiderophore metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to mugineic acid production and iron deficiency responses.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior pathway characterization summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms target product/precursor in phytosiderophore pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Iron deficiency-specific in grasses such as barley.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DOXC41 clade also contains H6H, highlighting unexpected phylogenetic relationships.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could alter metal homeostasis beyond iron.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IDS3 is a 2ODO in mugineic acid metabolism. 2. It contributes to iron deficiency-induced phytosiderophore production. 3. It is phylogenetically grouped with the unrelated alkaloid enzyme H6H.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for engineering scopolamine production in medicinal plants or microbial systems; should be balanced with upstream tropane alkaloid supply and toxicity management.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates hyoscyamine at the 6β position and supports conversion toward scopolamine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes scopolamine/tropane alkaloid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Classic biochemical characterization summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor / directs flux to target alkaloid
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to DOXC41 with IDS2/IDS3 despite unrelated pathway function.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher tropane alkaloids increase toxicity and may burden growth.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. H6H is a classic specialized-metabolism 2ODO in tropane alkaloid biosynthesis. 2. It hydroxylates hyoscyamine and channels flux toward scopolamine. 3. It exemplifies 2ODO recruitment into restricted medicinal alkaloid pathways.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for vindoline/vinca alkaloid pathway engineering in medicinal production systems.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates desacetoxyvindoline at the 4-position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes vindoline branch biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior biochemical characterization summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically grouped with maize BX6 in DOXC31.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Complex MIA pathway manipulation can create toxic intermediate accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D4H is a 2ODO in monoterpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis. 2. It hydroxylates desacetoxyvindoline in the vindoline branch. 3. Its phylogenetic relationship to unrelated enzymes illustrates 2ODO diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and pharmaceutical anticancer alkaloid precursor

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good target to manipulate maize defense metabolite spectrum; can be combined with BX7 for branch optimization.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation of DIMBOA-Glc leading to formation of TRIMBOA-Glc via unusual ortho-rearrangement of a methoxy group after C7 hydroxylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls flux to specific benzoxazinoid metabolites and supports alternative route to DIM2BOA-Glc through downstream methylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL mapping; recombinant enzyme assays; metabolite analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural variation among maize lines in benzoxazinoid levels.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistically notable due to methoxy-group 1,2-shift; enzyme was first expected to hydroxylate C8 but instead acts at C7.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing benzoxazinoids may modify defense spectrum and ecological interactions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BX13 was identified by QTL mapping as a benzoxazinoid-pathway 2ODO. 2. Recombinant enzyme assays showed unexpected C7 hydroxylation coupled to methoxy rearrangement, generating TRIMBOA-Glc. 3. BX13 revealed an alternative biosynthetic route in maize defense metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for defense pathway engineering in cereals.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an oxidation step in benzoxazinoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes benzoxazinoid metabolite formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior pathway characterization summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically related to D4H and GRS1 in DOXC31.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering defense chemistry may affect growth-defense tradeoffs.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BX6 is a benzoxazinoid-pathway 2ODO cited in the review. 2. It belongs to the same subclade as D4H and GRS1. 3. It exemplifies specialized recruitment of 2ODOs in cereal defense pathways.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering flavone accumulation and UV/defense traits; active-site engineering may favor desaturation over hydroxylation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates flavanones to flavones; some enzymes also hydroxylate flavanones to 2-hydroxynaringenin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavone formation such as apigenin; in bifunctional forms also produces 2-hydroxynaringenin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical assays; structural modelling; site-directed mutagenesis (e.g., Tyr240 in liverwort FNSI).
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified in liverwort, rice, Arabidopsis and maize.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review emphasizes that FNSI is more broadly distributed than previously thought, not restricted to Apiaceae; Y240P in liverwort ablates hydroxylation and leaves desaturation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Redirecting flux to flavones may reduce other flavonoid classes.
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Plagiochasma appendiculatum; Oryza sativa; Arabidopsis thaliana; Zea mays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FNSI catalyzes flavanone desaturation to flavones. 2. Some FNSI enzymes are bifunctional and also hydroxylate substrates. 3. Active-site residue Tyr240 is critical for hydroxylation/desaturation outcome in liverwort FNSI.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for modulating flavonoid branch flux toward dihydroflavonols and downstream pigments.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones to dihydroflavonols.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides precursor supply for flavonols and anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Established pathway biochemistry summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified in DOXC28 with FNSI.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Redirecting flux can alter pigmentation and stress protection.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F3H is a representative flavonoid-pathway 2ODO. 2. It generates dihydroflavonols from flavanones. 3. The review places F3H in the DOXC28 clade with FNSI.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to increase flavonols in crops, with possible effects on antioxidant capacity and pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates dihydroflavonols to flavonols.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Established pathway biochemistry summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified in DOXC47 with ANS.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May redirect flux away from anthocyanins or other flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLS is a representative 2ODO in the flavonol branch. 2. It converts dihydroflavonols to flavonols. 3. The review groups it with ANS in DOXC47.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for altering anthocyanin accumulation and pigmentation/stress traits; consider coupling with upstream flavonoid genes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Structural characterization with substrate analog and Fe2+ (PDB 1gp5); established pathway biochemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review uses AtANS as structural benchmark for plant 2ODOs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can alter pigmentation and carbon allocation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtANS is a structurally characterized plant 2ODO. 2. It catalyzes anthocyanidin formation in flavonoid metabolism. 3. Its structure informed modelling of other plant 2ODOs.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major target for modifying ripening, senescence and stress responses; however, broad pleiotropy requires context-specific manipulation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes ACC with O2 to yield ethylene, CO2 and cyanide via a non-2OG-dependent mechanism involving cyanoformate intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces ethylene hormone.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical mechanism studies; crystal structures from petunia and Arabidopsis; inhibitor studies.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CO2/bicarbonate requirement discussed; inhibitor pyrazinecarboxylic acid (POA) studies.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetic member of 2ODO superfamily but not 2OG-dependent; shares DSBH fold and facial triad-like active site features.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong effects on ripening, senescence, stress responses, and growth.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Divergent member of 2ODO superfamily with DSBH fold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACCO is a divergent 2ODO-superfamily enzyme that produces ethylene from ACC without 2OG. 2. Structural studies clarified substrate binding and inhibitor binding. 3. The mechanism likely uses a cyanoformate intermediate and Fe4+=O chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Many plant tissues including ripening/stressed tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ripening, senescence, stress and developmental signaling

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be used to alter aromatic amino acid catabolism and downstream plastoquinone/tocopherol precursor supply, though this review does not expand application.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate through dioxygenation chemistry in tyrosine catabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports tyrosine degradation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Listed as mechanistically similar comparator in review.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a true 2OG-dependent enzyme; structurally distinct but mechanistically similar to 2ODOs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May impact aromatic metabolism broadly.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Non-heme dioxygenase, mechanistically similar but phylogenetically distinct from 2ODOs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HPPD is included as a mechanistically related comparator. 2. It participates in tyrosine catabolism. 3. The review notes its structural distinction from canonical 2ODOs.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Amino acid catabolism and precursor supply

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for increasing tanshinones in medicinal root cultures; best deployed with root-specific expression and pathway balancing.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidation step(s) contributing to tanshinone formation; exact reaction not specified in the review excerpt.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing reduces several tanshinones, indicating positive contribution to pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome-wide phylogenetic survey; expression profiling; RNAi silencing in transgenic root culture; metabolite profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only one surveyed gene showed strong root-specific expression and functional evidence in tanshinone metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert diterpenoid flux or alter root metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Potential control point but not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A root-specific 2ODO, 2OGD5, was identified in danshen. 2. RNAi silencing reduced miltirone, cryptotanshinone and tanshinone IIA. 3. The gene therefore contributes positively to tanshinone biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential epigenetic lever to alter flowering/developmental programs, but broad chromatin effects make it unsuitable for targeted metabolic engineering without major pleiotropic risk.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Histone demethylation reprograms chromatin state rather than directly producing a metabolite; included in review as a 2ODO with epigenetic role.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior genetic and biochemical studies summarized in review.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent chromatin regulator, not a direct nutrient-metabolism determinant in this review.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: ELF6 itself is a histone lysine demethylase mediating epigenetic reprogramming.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Chromatin-mediated regulation rather than a specific metabolic pathway in this review.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review covers the full plant 2ODO superfamily; not a core nutrient-metabolism regulator for a terminal metabolite.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Extensive pleiotropy due to genome-wide chromatin effects.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JmjC-domain histone lysine demethylase, DOXB/related histone-modifying 2ODO group
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ELF6 is cited as a plant 2ODO involved in histone demethylation. 2. It exemplifies expansion of 2ODOs into epigenetic regulation. 3. Its role is regulatory rather than a direct metabolic biosynthetic step.
